ISBN 458213402-5. {{Kujūku Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|上野王子|version=34598366|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Municipalities in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Gobo, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Gobo, Wakayama]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] }} [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[en:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[ja:上野王子]] [[ja:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[zh:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[sn:eno Ōji (Gobo)]]

revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Kumano Three Shrines]] → [[Kumano shrine]]
revision.text:
'''Ueno Ōji (Gobo)''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|fr|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name=Ueno Ōji | image=[[File:Ichinono Oji haiden 2009-05-03.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall and Shrine Grove | location={{ill|Gobo|ja|御坊市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobo|qq=}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan | coordinates={{Coord|33|53|N|135|9|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Amitābha|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amitābha|qq=}}, {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō Bosatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音菩薩|1=Kannon|lt=Guanyin|2=ja|3=観音菩薩|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|13=User:Immanuelle/Guanyin|qq=}} | Historical Rank=One of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Designation=Gobo City Designated Historic Site }} '''Ueno Ōji''' (上野王子) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Gobo|ja|御坊市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobo|qq=}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakayama Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}} shrines. The site of the shrine is marked with a sign for Ueno Ōji at the outskirts of the Ueno settlement in Nata-chō, just before the bridge crossing the Uenogawa River along the old road near [[:en:Japan_National_Route_42|Japan National Route 42]]. It was designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on May 8, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/20.html | title=Site of Ueno Ōji | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == History == While the exact founding date of Ueno Ōji is unknown, it appears in historical records dating back to the early 12th century. In the {{ill|Chūyūki|ja|中右記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūyūki|qq=}}, an entry dated October 20, 1109 (Tennin 2) mentions a purification ritual performed at Ueno Slope. The shrine is first explicitly mentioned as "Next, Ueno Ōji" in an entry from October 10, 1201 (Kennin 1) in the "Kumano Dō no Aimagoki" (included in the {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}). Another record of a pilgrimage visit appears in the {{ill|Minkeiki|ja|民経記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minkeiki|qq=}} dated April 26, 1210 (Jōgen 4).Heibonsha [1997: 105] In early modern records, it is noted that a Buddhist well (仏井戸) located along the Oguri Kaidō within a field was considered the original site of the shrine (as mentioned in the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|qq=}}). Three Buddhist statues—{{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō Bosatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Amitābha|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amitābha|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音菩薩|1=Kannon|lt=Guanyin|2=ja|3=観音菩薩|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|13=User:Immanuelle/Guanyin|qq=}}Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|qq=}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|ja|熊野古道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kodō|qq=}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Kujūku Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kujūku Ōji (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Kujūku Ōji (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kujūku Ōji (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). ''Kumano Kōdō Michishirube - Current Survey of the Kumano Kujūku Ōji Sites''. Arao Seibundō (Minamoto Selection 1). '' Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). ''Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook''. Shinpū Shobō. ISBN 978-4882696292. '' Heibonsha (Ed.) (1997). ''Encyclopedia of Yamato and Kii Temples and Shrines''. Heibonsha. ISBN 458213402-5. {{Kujūku Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|上野王子|version=34598366|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Municipalities in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Gobo, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Gobo, Wakayama]] [[Category:Kujūku Ōji]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] }} [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[en:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[ja:上野王子]] [[ja:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[zh:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[sn:eno Ōji (Gobo)]]

Three Buddhist statues—{{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō Bosatsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Amitābha|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amitābha|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音菩薩|1=Kannon|lt=Guanyin|2=ja|3=観音菩薩|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|13=User:Immanuelle/Guanyin|qq=}}Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|qq=}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Kodō|ja|熊野古道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Kodō|qq=}} - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kujūku Ōji (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Kujūku Ōji (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kujūku Ōji (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). '''Ueno Ōji (Gobo)''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name=Ueno Ōji | image=[[File:Ichinono Oji haiden 2009-05-03.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall and Shrine Grove | location=[[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates={{Coord|33|53|N|135|9|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] | Historical Rank=One of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Designation=Gobo City Designated Historic Site }} '''Ueno Ōji''' (上野王子) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines. The site of the shrine is marked with a sign for Ueno Ōji at the outskirts of the Ueno settlement in Nata-chō, just before the bridge crossing the Uenogawa River along the old road near [[:en:Japan_National_Route_42|Japan National Route 42]]. It was designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on May 8, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/20.html | title=Site of Ueno Ōji | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == History == While the exact founding date of Ueno Ōji is unknown, it appears in historical records dating back to the early 12th century. In the {{ill|Chūyūki|ja|中右記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūyūki|qq=}}, an entry dated October 20, 1109 (Tennin 2) mentions a purification ritual performed at Ueno Slope. The shrine is first explicitly mentioned as "Next, Ueno Ōji" in an entry from October 10, 1201 (Kennin 1) in the "Kumano Dō no Aimagoki" (included in the {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}). Another record of a pilgrimage visit appears in the {{ill|Minkeiki|ja|民経記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minkeiki|qq=}} dated April 26, 1210 (Jōgen 4).Heibonsha [1997: 105] In early modern records, it is noted that a Buddhist well (仏井戸) located along the Oguri Kaidō within a field was considered the original site of the shrine (as mentioned in the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|qq=}}). Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|qq=}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kujūku Ōji (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Kujūku Ōji (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kujūku Ōji (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). ''Kumano Kōdō Michishirube - Current Survey of the Kumano Kujūku Ōji Sites''. Arao Seibundō (Minamoto Selection 1). '' Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). ''Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook''. Shinpū Shobō. ISBN 978-4882696292. '' Heibonsha (Ed.) (1997). ''Encyclopedia of Yamato and Kii Temples and Shrines''. Heibonsha. '''Ueno Ōji (Gobo)''' is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name=Ueno Ōji | image=[[File:Ichinono Oji haiden 2009-05-03.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall and Shrine Grove | location=[[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates={{Coord|33|53|N|135|9|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] | Historical Rank=One of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Designation=Gobo City Designated Historic Site }} '''Ueno Ōji''' (上野王子) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines. The site of the shrine is marked with a sign for Ueno Ōji at the outskirts of the Ueno settlement in Nata-chō, just before the bridge crossing the Uenogawa River along the old road near [[:en:Japan_National_Route_42|Japan National Route 42]]. It was designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on May 8, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/20.html | title=Site of Ueno Ōji | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == History == While the exact founding date of Ueno Ōji is unknown, it appears in historical records dating back to the early 12th century. In the {{ill|Chūyūki|ja|中右記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūyūki|qq=}}, an entry dated October 20, 1109 (Tennin 2) mentions a purification ritual performed at Ueno Slope. The shrine is first explicitly mentioned as "Next, Ueno Ōji" in an entry from October 10, 1201 (Kennin 1) in the "Kumano Dō no Aimagoki" (included in the {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}). Another record of a pilgrimage visit appears in the {{ill|Minkeiki|ja|民経記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minkeiki|qq=}} dated April 26, 1210 (Jōgen 4).Heibonsha [1997: 105] In early modern records, it is noted that a Buddhist well (仏井戸) located along the Oguri Kaidō within a field was considered the original site of the shrine (as mentioned in the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|qq=}}). Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|qq=}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujūku Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kujūku Ōji (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Kujūku Ōji (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Kujūku Ōji (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kujūku Ōji (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). ''Kumano Kōdō Michishirube - Current Survey of the Kumano Kujūku Ōji Sites''. Arao Seibundō (Minamoto Selection 1). '' Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). ''Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook''. Shinpū Shobō. ISBN 978-4882696292. '' Heibonsha (Ed.) (1997). ''Encyclopedia of Yamato and Kii Temples and Shrines''. Heibonsha. revision.text:
'''Ueno Ōji (Gobo)''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name=Ueno Ōji | image=[[File:Ichinono Oji haiden 2009-05-03.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall and Shrine Grove | location=[[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates={{Coord|33|53|N|135|9|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] | Historical Rank=One of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Designation=Gobo City Designated Historic Site }} '''Ueno Ōji''' (上野王子) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines. The site of the shrine is marked with a sign for Ueno Ōji at the outskirts of the Ueno settlement in Nata-chō, just before the bridge crossing the Uenogawa River along the old road near [[:en:Japan_National_Route_42|Japan National Route 42]]. It was designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on May 8, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/20.html | title=Site of Ueno Ōji | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == History == While the exact founding date of Ueno Ōji is unknown, it appears in historical records dating back to the early 12th century. In the {{ill|Chūyūki|ja|中右記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūyūki|qq=}}, an entry dated October 20, 1109 (Tennin 2) mentions a purification ritual performed at Ueno Slope. The shrine is first explicitly mentioned as "Next, Ueno Ōji" in an entry from October 10, 1201 (Kennin 1) in the "Kumano Dō no Aimagoki" (included in the {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}). Another record of a pilgrimage visit appears in the {{ill|Minkeiki|ja|民経記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minkeiki|qq=}} dated April 26, 1210 (Jōgen 4).Heibonsha [1997: 105] In early modern records, it is noted that a Buddhist well (仏井戸) located along the Oguri Kaidō within a field was considered the original site of the shrine (as mentioned in the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|qq=}}). Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|qq=}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). ''Kumano Kōdō Michishirube - Current Survey of the Kumano Ninety-Nine Ōji Sites''. Arao Seibundō (Minamoto Selection 1). '' Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). ''Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook''. Shinpū Shobō. ISBN 978-4882696292. '' Heibonsha (Ed.) (1997). ''Encyclopedia of Yamato and Kii Temples and Shrines''. Heibonsha. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). revision.text:
'''Ueno Ōji (Gobo)''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name=Ueno Ōji | image=[[File:Ichinono Oji haiden 2009-05-03.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall and Shrine Grove | location=[[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates={{Coord|33|53|N|135|9|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] | Historical Rank=One of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Designation=Gobo City Designated Historic Site }} '''Ueno Ōji''' (上野王子) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines. The site of the shrine is marked with a sign for Ueno Ōji at the outskirts of the Ueno settlement in Nata-chō, just before the bridge crossing the Uenogawa River along the old road near [[:en:Japan_National_Route_42|Japan National Route 42]]. It was designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on May 8, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/20.html | title=Site of Ueno Ōji | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == History == While the exact founding date of Ueno Ōji is unknown, it appears in historical records dating back to the early 12th century. In the {{ill|Chūyūki|ja|中右記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūyūki|qq=}}, an entry dated October 20, 1109 (Tennin 2) mentions a purification ritual performed at Ueno Slope. The shrine is first explicitly mentioned as "Next, Ueno Ōji" in an entry from October 10, 1201 (Kennin 1) in the "Kumano Dō no Aimagoki" (included in the {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}). Another record of a pilgrimage visit appears in the {{ill|Minkeiki|ja|民経記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minkeiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広橋経光}} dated April 26, 1210 (Jōgen 4).Heibonsha [1997: 105] In early modern records, it is noted that a Buddhist well (仏井戸) located along the Oguri Kaidō within a field was considered the original site of the shrine (as mentioned in the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|qq=}}). Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). ''Kumano Kōdō Michishirube - Current Survey of the Kumano Ninety-Nine Ōji Sites''. Arao Seibundō (Minamoto Selection 1). '' Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). ''Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook''. Shinpū Shobō. ISBN 978-4882696292. '' Heibonsha (Ed.) (1997). ''Encyclopedia of Yamato and Kii Temples and Shrines''. Heibonsha. ISBN 458213402-5. {{Ninety-Nine Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|上野王子|version=34598366|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Municipalities in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Gobo, Wakayama]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] }} [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[ja:上野王子]] [[ja:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[zh:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[sn:eno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]]

In the {{ill|Chūyūki|ja|中右記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūyūki|qq=}}, an entry dated October 20, 1109 (Tennin 2) mentions a purification ritual performed at Ueno Slope. The shrine is first explicitly mentioned as "Next, Ueno Ōji" in an entry from October 10, 1201 (Kennin 1) in the "Kumano Dō no Aimagoki" (included in the {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}). Another record of a pilgrimage visit appears in the {{ill|Minkeiki|ja|民経記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minkeiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広橋経光}} dated April 26, 1210 (Jōgen 4).Heibonsha [1997: 105] In early modern records, it is noted that a Buddhist well (仏井戸) located along the Oguri Kaidō within a field was considered the original site of the shrine (as mentioned in the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|qq=}}). Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). ''Kumano Kōdō Michishirube - Current Survey of the Kumano Ninety-Nine Ōji Sites''. Arao Seibundō (Minamoto Selection 1). '' Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). ''Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook''. Shinpū Shobō. ISBN 978-4882696292. '' Heibonsha (Ed.) (1997). ''Encyclopedia of Yamato and Kii Temples and Shrines''. Heibonsha. ISBN 458213402-5. {{Ninety-Nine Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|上野王子|version=34598366|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] }} [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[ja:上野王子]] [[ja:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[zh:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[sn:eno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11360401]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ueno Ōji (Gobo)''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name=Ueno Ōji | image=[[File:Ichinono Oji haiden 2009-05-03.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall and Shrine Grove | location=[[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates={{Coord|33|53|N|135|9|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] | Historical Rank=One of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Designation=Gobo City Designated Historic Site }} '''Ueno Ōji''' (上野王子) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines. The site of the shrine is marked with a sign for Ueno Ōji at the outskirts of the Ueno settlement in Nata-chō, just before the bridge crossing the Uenogawa River along the old road near [[:en:Japan_National_Route_42|Japan National Route 42]]. It was designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on May 8, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/20.html | title=Site of Ueno Ōji | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == History == While the exact founding date of Ueno Ōji is unknown, it appears in historical records dating back to the early 12th century. In the {{ill|Chūyūki|ja|中右記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūyūki|qq=}}, an entry dated October 20, 1109 (Tennin 2) mentions a purification ritual performed at Ueno Slope. The shrine is first explicitly mentioned as "Next, Ueno Ōji" in an entry from October 10, 1201 (Kennin 1) in the "Kumano Dō no Aimagoki" (included in the {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}). Another record of a pilgrimage visit appears in the {{ill|Minkeiki|ja|民経記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minkeiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広橋経光}} dated April 26, 1210 (Jōgen 4).Heibonsha [1997: 105] In early modern records, it is noted that a Buddhist well (仏井戸) located along the Oguri Kaidō within a field was considered the original site of the shrine (as mentioned in the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|qq=}}). Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). ''Kumano Kōdō Michishirube - Current Survey of the Kumano Ninety-Nine Ōji Sites''. Arao Seibundō (Minamoto Selection 1). '' Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). ''Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook''. Shinpū Shobō. ISBN 978-4882696292. '' Heibonsha (Ed.) (1997). ''Encyclopedia of Yamato and Kii Temples and Shrines''. Heibonsha. ISBN 458213402-5. {{Ninety-Nine Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|上野王子|version=34598366|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] }} [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[ja:上野王子]] [[ja:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[zh:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[sn:eno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). Shinpū Shobō. ISBN 978-4882696292. '' Heibonsha (Ed.) (1997). ''Encyclopedia of Yamato and Kii Temples and Shrines''. Heibonsha. ISBN 458213402-5. {{Ninety-Nine Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|上野王子|version=34598366|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] }} [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[ja:上野王子]] [[ja:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[zh:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[sn:eno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11360401]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ueno Ōji (Gobo)''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name=Ueno Ōji | image=[[File:Ichinono Oji haiden 2009-05-03.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall and Shrine Grove | location=[[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates={{Coord|33|53|N|135|9|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] | Historical Rank=One of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Designation=Gobo City Designated Historic Site }} '''Ueno Ōji''' (上野王子) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines. The site of the shrine is marked with a sign for Ueno Ōji at the outskirts of the Ueno settlement in Nata-chō, just before the bridge crossing the Uenogawa River along the old road near [[:en:Japan_National_Route_42|Japan National Route 42]]. It was designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on May 8, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/20.html | title=Site of Ueno Ōji | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == History == While the exact founding date of Ueno Ōji is unknown, it appears in historical records dating back to the early 12th century. In the {{ill|Chūyūki|ja|中右記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūyūki|qq=}}, an entry dated October 20, 1109 (Tennin 2) mentions a purification ritual performed at Ueno Slope. The shrine is first explicitly mentioned as "Next, Ueno Ōji" in an entry from October 10, 1201 (Kennin 1) in the "Kumano Dō no Aimagoki" (included in the {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}). Another record of a pilgrimage visit appears in the {{ill|Minkeiki|ja|民経記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minkeiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広橋経光}} dated April 26, 1210 (Jōgen 4).Heibonsha [1997: 105] In early modern records, it is noted that a Buddhist well (仏井戸) located along the Oguri Kaidō within a field was considered the original site of the shrine (as mentioned in the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|qq=}}). Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). ''Kumano Kōdō Michishirube - Current Survey of the Kumano Ninety-Nine Ōji Sites''. Arao Seibundō (Minamoto Selection 1). '' Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). ''Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook''. Shinpū Shobō. ISBN 978-4882696292. '' Heibonsha (Ed.) (1997). ''Encyclopedia of Yamato and Kii Temples and Shrines''. Heibonsha. ISBN 458213402-5. {{Ninety-Nine Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|上野王子|version=34598366|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] }} [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[ja:上野王子]] [[ja:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[zh:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[sn:eno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[Category:qq]]

It is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines. The site of the shrine is marked with a sign for Ueno Ōji at the outskirts of the Ueno settlement in Nata-chō, just before the bridge crossing the Uenogawa River along the old road near [[:en:Japan_National_Route_42|Japan National Route 42]]. It was designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on May 8, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/20.html | title=Site of Ueno Ōji | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == History == While the exact founding date of Ueno Ōji is unknown, it appears in historical records dating back to the early 12th century. In the {{ill|Chūyūki|ja|中右記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūyūki|qq=}}, an entry dated October 20, 1109 (Tennin 2) mentions a purification ritual performed at Ueno Slope. The shrine is first explicitly mentioned as "Next, Ueno Ōji" in an entry from October 10, 1201 (Kennin 1) in the "Kumano Dō no Aimagoki" (included in the {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}). Another record of a pilgrimage visit appears in the {{ill|Minkeiki|ja|民経記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minkeiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広橋経光|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} dated April 26, 1210 (Jōgen 4).Heibonsha [1997: 105] In early modern records, it is noted that a Buddhist well (仏井戸) located along the Oguri Kaidō within a field was considered the original site of the shrine (as mentioned in the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|qq=|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}). Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). Main Hall and Shrine Grove | location=[[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates={{Coord|33|53|N|135|9|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] | Historical Rank=One of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Designation=Gobo City Designated Historic Site }} '''Ueno Ōji''' (上野王子) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines. The site of the shrine is marked with a sign for Ueno Ōji at the outskirts of the Ueno settlement in Nata-chō, just before the bridge crossing the Uenogawa River along the old road near [[:en:Japan_National_Route_42|Japan National Route 42]]. It was designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on May 8, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/20.html | title=Site of Ueno Ōji | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == History == While the exact founding date of Ueno Ōji is unknown, it appears in historical records dating back to the early 12th century. In the {{ill|Chūyūki|ja|中右記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūyūki|qq=}}, an entry dated October 20, 1109 (Tennin 2) mentions a purification ritual performed at Ueno Slope. The shrine is first explicitly mentioned as "Next, Ueno Ōji" in an entry from October 10, 1201 (Kennin 1) in the "Kumano Dō no Aimagoki" (included in the {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}). Another record of a pilgrimage visit appears in the {{ill|Minkeiki|ja|民経記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minkeiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広橋経光|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} dated April 26, 1210 (Jōgen 4).Heibonsha [1997: 105] In early modern records, it is noted that a Buddhist well (仏井戸) located along the Oguri Kaidō within a field was considered the original site of the shrine (as mentioned in the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|qq=|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}). Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). ''Kumano Kōdō Michishirube - Current Survey of the Kumano Ninety-Nine Ōji Sites''. Arao Seibundō (Minamoto Selection 1). '' Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). ''Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook''. Shinpū Shobō. ISBN 978-4882696292. '' Heibonsha (Ed.) (1997). ''Encyclopedia of Yamato and Kii Temples and Shrines''. Heibonsha. ISBN 458213402-5. {{Ninety-Nine Ōji}} {{translated page|ja|上野王子|version=34598366|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] }} [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[ja:上野王子]] [[ja:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[zh:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[sn:eno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360402]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ueno Ōji (Gobo)''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name=Ueno Ōji | image=[[File:Ichinono Oji haiden 2009-05-03.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall and Shrine Grove | location=[[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates={{Coord|33|53|N|135|9|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] | Historical Rank=One of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Designation=Gobo City Designated Historic Site }} '''Ueno Ōji''' (上野王子) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines. The site of the shrine is marked with a sign for Ueno Ōji at the outskirts of the Ueno settlement in Nata-chō, just before the bridge crossing the Uenogawa River along the old road near [[:en:Japan_National_Route_42|Japan National Route 42]]. It was designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on May 8, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/20.html | title=Site of Ueno Ōji | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == History == While the exact founding date of Ueno Ōji is unknown, it appears in historical records dating back to the early 12th century. In the {{ill|Chūyūki|ja|中右記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūyūki|qq=}}, an entry dated October 20, 1109 (Tennin 2) mentions a purification ritual performed at Ueno Slope. The shrine is first explicitly mentioned as "Next, Ueno Ōji" in an entry from October 10, 1201 (Kennin 1) in the "Kumano Dō no Aimagoki" (included in the {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}). Another record of a pilgrimage visit appears in the {{ill|Minkeiki|ja|民経記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minkeiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広橋経光|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} dated April 26, 1210 (Jōgen 4).Heibonsha [1997: 105] In early modern records, it is noted that a Buddhist well (仏井戸) located along the Oguri Kaidō within a field was considered the original site of the shrine (as mentioned in the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|qq=|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}). Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). ''Kumano Kōdō Michishirube - Current Survey of the Kumano Ninety-Nine Ōji Sites''. Arao Seibundō (Minamoto Selection 1). '' Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). ''Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook''. Shinpū Shobō. ISBN 978-4882696292. '' Heibonsha (Ed.) (1997). ''Encyclopedia of Yamato and Kii Temples and Shrines''. Heibonsha. ISBN 458213402-5. {{Ninety-Nine Ōji}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] }} [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[ja:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[zh:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[sn:eno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360402]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ueno Ōji (Gobo)''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name=Ueno Ōji | image=[[File:Ichinono Oji haiden 2009-05-03.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall and Shrine Grove | location=[[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates={{Coord|33|53|N|135|9|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] | Historical Rank=One of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Designation=Gobo City Designated Historic Site }} '''Ueno Ōji''' (上野王子) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines. The site of the shrine is marked with a sign for Ueno Ōji at the outskirts of the Ueno settlement in Nata-chō, just before the bridge crossing the Uenogawa River along the old road near [[:en:Japan_National_Route_42|Japan National Route 42]]. It was designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on May 8, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/20.html | title=Site of Ueno Ōji | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == History == While the exact founding date of Ueno Ōji is unknown, it appears in historical records dating back to the early 12th century. In the {{ill|Chūyūki|ja|中右記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūyūki|qq=}}, an entry dated October 20, 1109 (Tennin 2) mentions a purification ritual performed at Ueno Slope. The shrine is first explicitly mentioned as "Next, Ueno Ōji" in an entry from October 10, 1201 (Kennin 1) in the "Kumano Dō no Aimagoki" (included in the {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}). Another record of a pilgrimage visit appears in the {{ill|Minkeiki|ja|民経記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minkeiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広橋経光|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} dated April 26, 1210 (Jōgen 4).Heibonsha [1997: 105] In early modern records, it is noted that a Buddhist well (仏井戸) located along the Oguri Kaidō within a field was considered the original site of the shrine (as mentioned in the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|qq=|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}). Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrine (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrine (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrine (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrine (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrine (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). ''Kumano Kōdō Michishirube - Current Survey of the Kumano Ninety-Nine Ōji Sites''. Arao Seibundō (Minamoto Selection 1). '' Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). ''Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook''. Shinpū Shobō. ISBN 978-4882696292. '' Heibonsha (Ed.) (1997). ''Encyclopedia of Yamato and Kii Temples and Shrines''. Heibonsha. ISBN 458213402-5. {{Ninety-Nine Ōji}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] }} [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[ja:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[zh:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[sn:eno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]]

The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). Shinpū Shobō. ISBN 978-4882696292. '' Heibonsha (Ed.) (1997). ''Encyclopedia of Yamato and Kii Temples and Shrines''. Heibonsha.
