{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360402]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ueno Ōji (Gobo)''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name=Ueno Ōji | image=[[File:Ichinono Oji haiden 2009-05-03.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall and Shrine Grove | location=[[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates={{Coord|33|53|N|135|9|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] | Historical Rank=One of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Designation=Gobo City Designated Historic Site }} '''Ueno Ōji''' (上野王子) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines. The site of the shrine is marked with a sign for Ueno Ōji at the outskirts of the Ueno settlement in Nata-chō, just before the bridge crossing the Uenogawa River along the old road near [[:en:Japan_National_Route_42|Japan National Route 42]]. It was designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on May 8, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/20.html | title=Site of Ueno Ōji | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == History == While the exact founding date of Ueno Ōji is unknown, it appears in historical records dating back to the early 12th century. In the {{ill|Chūyūki|ja|中右記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūyūki|qq=}}, an entry dated October 20, 1109 (Tennin 2) mentions a purification ritual performed at Ueno Slope. The shrine is first explicitly mentioned as "Next, Ueno Ōji" in an entry from October 10, 1201 (Kennin 1) in the "Kumano Dō no Aimagoki" (included in the {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}). Another record of a pilgrimage visit appears in the {{ill|Minkeiki|ja|民経記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minkeiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広橋経光|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} dated April 26, 1210 (Jōgen 4).Heibonsha [1997: 105] In early modern records, it is noted that a Buddhist well (仏井戸) located along the Oguri Kaidō within a field was considered the original site of the shrine (as mentioned in the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|qq=|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}). Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). ''Kumano Kōdō Michishirube - Current Survey of the Kumano Ninety-Nine Ōji Sites''. Arao Seibundō (Minamoto Selection 1). '' Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). ''Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook''. Shinpū Shobō. ISBN 978-4882696292. '' Heibonsha (Ed.) (1997). ''Encyclopedia of Yamato and Kii Temples and Shrines''. Heibonsha. ISBN 458213402-5. {{Ninety-Nine Ōji}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] }} [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[ja:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[zh:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[sn:eno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]]

Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360402]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ueno Ōji (Gobo)''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name=Ueno Ōji | image=[[File:Ichinono Oji haiden 2009-05-03.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall and Shrine Grove | location=[[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates={{Coord|33|53|N|135|9|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] | Historical Rank=One of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Designation=Gobo City Designated Historic Site }} '''Ueno Ōji''' (上野王子) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines. The site of the shrine is marked with a sign for Ueno Ōji at the outskirts of the Ueno settlement in Nata-chō, just before the bridge crossing the Uenogawa River along the old road near [[:en:Japan_National_Route_42|Japan National Route 42]]. It was designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on May 8, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/20.html | title=Site of Ueno Ōji | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == History == While the exact founding date of Ueno Ōji is unknown, it appears in historical records dating back to the early 12th century. In the {{ill|Chūyūki|ja|中右記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūyūki|qq=}}, an entry dated October 20, 1109 (Tennin 2) mentions a purification ritual performed at Ueno Slope. The shrine is first explicitly mentioned as "Next, Ueno Ōji" in an entry from October 10, 1201 (Kennin 1) in the "Kumano Dō no Aimagoki" (included in the {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}). Another record of a pilgrimage visit appears in the {{ill|Minkeiki|ja|民経記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minkeiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広橋経光|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} dated April 26, 1210 (Jōgen 4).Heibonsha [1997: 105] In early modern records, it is noted that a Buddhist well (仏井戸) located along the Oguri Kaidō within a field was considered the original site of the shrine (as mentioned in the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|qq=|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}). Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). ''Kumano Kōdō Michishirube - Current Survey of the Kumano Ninety-Nine Ōji Sites''. Arao Seibundō (Minamoto Selection 1). '' Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). ''Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook''. Shinpū Shobō. ISBN 978-4882696292. '' Heibonsha (Ed.) (1997). ''Encyclopedia of Yamato and Kii Temples and Shrines''. Heibonsha. ISBN 458213402-5. {{Ninety-Nine Ōji}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] }} [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[ja:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[zh:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[sn:eno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]]

The site of the shrine is marked with a sign for Ueno Ōji at the outskirts of the Ueno settlement in Nata-chō, just before the bridge crossing the Uenogawa River along the old road near [[:en:Japan_National_Route_42|Japan National Route 42]]. The shrine is first explicitly mentioned as "Next, Ueno Ōji" in an entry from October 10, 1201 (Kennin 1) in the "Kumano Dō no Aimagoki" (included in the {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}). The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). In the {{ill|Chūyūki|ja|中右記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūyūki|qq=}}, an entry dated October 20, 1109 (Tennin 2) mentions a purification ritual performed at Ueno Slope. The shrine is first explicitly mentioned as "Next, Ueno Ōji" in an entry from October 10, 1201 (Kennin 1) in the "Kumano Dō no Aimagoki" (included in the {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}). Another record of a pilgrimage visit appears in the {{ill|Minkeiki|ja|民経記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minkeiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広橋経光|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} dated April 26, 1210 (Jōgen 4).Heibonsha [1997: 105] In early modern records, it is noted that a Buddhist well (仏井戸) located along the Oguri Kaidō within a field was considered the original site of the shrine (as mentioned in the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|qq=|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}). Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). Main Hall and Shrine Grove | location=[[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates={{Coord|33|53|N|135|9|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] | Historical Rank=One of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Designation=Gobo City Designated Historic Site }} '''Ueno Ōji''' (上野王子) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines. The site of the shrine is marked with a sign for Ueno Ōji at the outskirts of the Ueno settlement in Nata-chō, just before the bridge crossing the Uenogawa River along the old road near [[:en:Japan_National_Route_42|Japan National Route 42]]. It was designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on May 8, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/20.html | title=Site of Ueno Ōji | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == History == While the exact founding date of Ueno Ōji is unknown, it appears in historical records dating back to the early 12th century. In the {{ill|Chūyūki|ja|中右記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūyūki|qq=}}, an entry dated October 20, 1109 (Tennin 2) mentions a purification ritual performed at Ueno Slope. The shrine is first explicitly mentioned as "Next, Ueno Ōji" in an entry from October 10, 1201 (Kennin 1) in the "Kumano Dō no Aimagoki" (included in the {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}). Another record of a pilgrimage visit appears in the {{ill|Minkeiki|ja|民経記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minkeiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広橋経光|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} dated April 26, 1210 (Jōgen 4).Heibonsha [1997: 105] In early modern records, it is noted that a Buddhist well (仏井戸) located along the Oguri Kaidō within a field was considered the original site of the shrine (as mentioned in the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|qq=|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}). Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360402]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ueno Ōji (Gobo)''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name=Ueno Ōji | image=[[File:Ichinono Oji haiden 2009-05-03.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall and Shrine Grove | location=[[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates={{Coord|33|53|N|135|9|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] | Historical Rank=One of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Designation=Gobo City Designated Historic Site }} '''Ueno Ōji''' (上野王子) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines. The site of the shrine is marked with a sign for Ueno Ōji at the outskirts of the Ueno settlement in Nata-chō, just before the bridge crossing the Uenogawa River along the old road near [[:en:Japan_National_Route_42|Japan National Route 42]]. It was designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on May 8, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/20.html | title=Site of Ueno Ōji | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == History == While the exact founding date of Ueno Ōji is unknown, it appears in historical records dating back to the early 12th century. In the {{ill|Chūyūki|ja|中右記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūyūki|qq=}}, an entry dated October 20, 1109 (Tennin 2) mentions a purification ritual performed at Ueno Slope. The shrine is first explicitly mentioned as "Next, Ueno Ōji" in an entry from October 10, 1201 (Kennin 1) in the "Kumano Dō no Aimagoki" (included in the {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}). Another record of a pilgrimage visit appears in the {{ill|Minkeiki|ja|民経記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minkeiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広橋経光|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} dated April 26, 1210 (Jōgen 4).Heibonsha [1997: 105] In early modern records, it is noted that a Buddhist well (仏井戸) located along the Oguri Kaidō within a field was considered the original site of the shrine (as mentioned in the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|qq=|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}). Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). ''Kumano Kōdō Michishirube - Current Survey of the Kumano Ninety-Nine Ōji Sites''. Arao Seibundō (Minamoto Selection 1). '' Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). ''Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook''. Shinpū Shobō. ISBN 978-4882696292. '' Heibonsha (Ed.) (1997). ''Encyclopedia of Yamato and Kii Temples and Shrines''. Heibonsha. ISBN 458213402-5. {{Ninety-Nine Ōji}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]][[sn:-てんふれえと]] }} [[sn:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[sn:-てんふれえと]] [[ja:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[zh:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[sn:eno Ōji (Gobo)]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[sn:Ueno Ōji Shrine (Gobo)]][[sn:Ueno Ōji Shrine (Gobo)]][[sn:Ueno Ōji Shrine (Gobo)]][[en:Ueno Ōji (Gobo)]] {{wikidata link|Q11360402}}

The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. Main Hall and Shrine Grove | location=[[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates={{Coord|33|53|N|135|9|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] | Historical Rank=One of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Designation=Gobo City Designated Historic Site }} '''Ueno Ōji''' (上野王子) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines. The site of the shrine is marked with a sign for Ueno Ōji at the outskirts of the Ueno settlement in Nata-chō, just before the bridge crossing the Uenogawa River along the old road near [[:en:Japan_National_Route_42|Japan National Route 42]]. It was designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on May 8, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/20.html | title=Site of Ueno Ōji | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == History == While the exact founding date of Ueno Ōji is unknown, it appears in historical records dating back to the early 12th century. In the {{ill|Chūyūki|ja|中右記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūyūki|qq=}}, an entry dated October 20, 1109 (Tennin 2) mentions a purification ritual performed at Ueno Slope. The shrine is first explicitly mentioned as "Next, Ueno Ōji" in an entry from October 10, 1201 (Kennin 1) in the "Kumano Dō no Aimagoki" (included in the {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}). Another record of a pilgrimage visit appears in the {{ill|Minkeiki|ja|民経記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minkeiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広橋経光|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} dated April 26, 1210 (Jōgen 4).Heibonsha [1997: 105] In early modern records, it is noted that a Buddhist well (仏井戸) located along the Oguri Kaidō within a field was considered the original site of the shrine (as mentioned in the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|qq=|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}). Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). ''Kumano Kōdō Michishirube - Current Survey of the Kumano Ninety-Nine Ōji Sites''. Arao Seibundō (Minamoto Selection 1). '' Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). ''Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook''. Shinpū Shobō. ISBN 978-4882696292. '' Heibonsha (Ed.) (1997). ''Encyclopedia of Yamato and Kii Temples and Shrines''. Heibonsha. ISBN 458213402-5. {{Ninety-Nine Ōji}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines]] }} [[ja:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[zh:上野王子 (御坊市)]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11360402}}

Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). [[ja:上野王子 (御坊市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360402]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ueno Ōji (Gobo)''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name=Ueno Ōji | image=[[File:Ichinono Oji haiden 2009-05-03.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall and Shrine Grove | location=[[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates={{Coord|33|53|N|135|9|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] | Historical Rank=One of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Designation=Gobo City Designated Historic Site }} '''Ueno Ōji''' (上野王子) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines. The site of the shrine is marked with a sign for Ueno Ōji at the outskirts of the Ueno settlement in Nata-chō, just before the bridge crossing the Uenogawa River along the old road near [[:en:Japan_National_Route_42|Japan National Route 42]]. It was designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on May 8, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/20.html | title=Site of Ueno Ōji | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == History == While the exact founding date of Ueno Ōji is unknown, it appears in historical records dating back to the early 12th century. In the {{ill|Chūyūki|ja|中右記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūyūki|qq=}}, an entry dated October 20, 1109 (Tennin 2) mentions a purification ritual performed at Ueno Slope. The shrine is first explicitly mentioned as "Next, Ueno Ōji" in an entry from October 10, 1201 (Kennin 1) in the "Kumano Dō no Aimagoki" (included in the {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}). Another record of a pilgrimage visit appears in the {{ill|Minkeiki|ja|民経記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minkeiki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広橋経光|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} dated April 26, 1210 (Jōgen 4).Heibonsha [1997: 105] In early modern records, it is noted that a Buddhist well (仏井戸) located along the Oguri Kaidō within a field was considered the original site of the shrine (as mentioned in the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|qq=|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}). Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|qid=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). Shinpū Shobō. ISBN 978-4882696292. '' Heibonsha (Ed.) (1997). ''Encyclopedia of Yamato and Kii Temples and Shrines''. Heibonsha. [[ja:上野王子 (御坊市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360402]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ueno Ōji (Gobo)''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name=Ueno Ōji | image=[[File:Ichinono Oji haiden 2009-05-03.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall and Shrine Grove | location=[[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates={{Coord|33|53|N|135|9|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] | Historical Rank=One of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Designation=Gobo City Designated Historic Site }} '''Ueno Ōji''' (上野王子) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines. The site of the shrine is marked with a sign for Ueno Ōji at the outskirts of the Ueno settlement in Nata-chō, just before the bridge crossing the Uenogawa River along the old road near [[:en:Japan_National_Route_42|Japan National Route 42]]. It was designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on May 8, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/20.html | title=Site of Ueno Ōji | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == History == While the exact founding date of Ueno Ōji is unknown, it appears in historical records dating back to the early 12th century. In the {{ill|Chūyūki|ja|中右記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūyūki|qq=}}, an entry dated October 20, 1109 (Tennin 2) mentions a purification ritual performed at Ueno Slope. The shrine is first explicitly mentioned as "Next, Ueno Ōji" in an entry from October 10, 1201 (Kennin 1) in the "Kumano Dō no Aimagoki" (included in the {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}). Another record of a pilgrimage visit appears in the {{ill|Minkeiki|ja|民経記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minkeiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広橋経光|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} dated April 26, 1210 (Jōgen 4).Heibonsha [1997: 105] In early modern records, it is noted that a Buddhist well (仏井戸) located along the Oguri Kaidō within a field was considered the original site of the shrine (as mentioned in the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}). Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|qq=draft|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|qq=draft|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|oji=王子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). Shinpū Shobō. ISBN 978-4882696292. '' Heibonsha (Ed.) (1997). ''Encyclopedia of Yamato and Kii Temples and Shrines''. Heibonsha. The shrine is first explicitly mentioned as "Next, Ueno Ōji" in an entry from October 10, 1201 (Kennin 1) in the "Kumano Dō no Aimagoki" (included in the {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}). Another record of a pilgrimage visit appears in the {{ill|Minkeiki|ja|民経記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minkeiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広橋経光|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} dated April 26, 1210 (Jōgen 4).Heibonsha [1997: 105] In early modern records, it is noted that a Buddhist well (仏井戸) located along the Oguri Kaidō within a field was considered the original site of the shrine (as mentioned in the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}). Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|oji=王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). Main Hall and Shrine Grove | location=[[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates={{Coord|33|53|N|135|9|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] | Historical Rank=One of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Designation=Gobo City Designated Historic Site }} '''Ueno Ōji''' (上野王子) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines. The site of the shrine is marked with a sign for Ueno Ōji at the outskirts of the Ueno settlement in Nata-chō, just before the bridge crossing the Uenogawa River along the old road near [[:en:Japan_National_Route_42|Japan National Route 42]]. It was designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on May 8, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/20.html | title=Site of Ueno Ōji | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == History == While the exact founding date of Ueno Ōji is unknown, it appears in historical records dating back to the early 12th century. In the {{ill|Chūyūki|ja|中右記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūyūki|qq=}}, an entry dated October 20, 1109 (Tennin 2) mentions a purification ritual performed at Ueno Slope. The shrine is first explicitly mentioned as "Next, Ueno Ōji" in an entry from October 10, 1201 (Kennin 1) in the "Kumano Dō no Aimagoki" (included in the {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}). Another record of a pilgrimage visit appears in the {{ill|Minkeiki|ja|民経記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minkeiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広橋経光|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} dated April 26, 1210 (Jōgen 4).Heibonsha [1997: 105] In early modern records, it is noted that a Buddhist well (仏井戸) located along the Oguri Kaidō within a field was considered the original site of the shrine (as mentioned in the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}). Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|oji=王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). Main Hall and Shrine Grove | location=[[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates={{Coord|33|53|N|135|9|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] | Historical Rank=One of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Designation=Gobo City Designated Historic Site }} '''Ueno Ōji''' (上野王子) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines. The site of the shrine is marked with a sign for Ueno Ōji at the outskirts of the Ueno settlement in Nata-chō, just before the bridge crossing the Uenogawa River along the old road near [[:en:Japan_National_Route_42|Japan National Route 42]]. It was designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on May 8, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/20.html | title=Site of Ueno Ōji | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == History == While the exact founding date of Ueno Ōji is unknown, it appears in historical records dating back to the early 12th century. In the {{ill|Chūyūki|ja|中右記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūyūki|qq=}}, an entry dated October 20, 1109 (Tennin 2) mentions a purification ritual performed at Ueno Slope. The shrine is first explicitly mentioned as "Next, Ueno Ōji" in an entry from October 10, 1201 (Kennin 1) in the "Kumano Dō no Aimagoki" (included in the {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}). Another record of a pilgrimage visit appears in the {{ill|Minkeiki|ja|民経記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minkeiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広橋経光|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} dated April 26, 1210 (Jōgen 4).Heibonsha [1997: 105] In early modern records, it is noted that a Buddhist well (仏井戸) located along the Oguri Kaidō within a field was considered the original site of the shrine (as mentioned in the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}). Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|oji=王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). ''Kumano Kōdō Michishirube - Current Survey of the Kumano Ninety-Nine Ōji Sites''. Arao Seibundō (Minamoto Selection 1). '' Hasegawa, Yasutaka (2007). ''Kumano Ōji Pilgrimage Guidebook''. Shinpū Shobō. ISBN 978-4882696292. '' Heibonsha (Ed.) (1997). ''Encyclopedia of Yamato and Kii Temples and Shrines''. Heibonsha. Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|oji=王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987). [[ja:上野王子 (御坊市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11360402]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ueno Ōji (Gobo)''' is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=99 ōji Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} on the [[Kumano Kodō]]. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name=Ueno Ōji | image=[[File:Ichinono Oji haiden 2009-05-03.jpg|250px]]
Main Hall and Shrine Grove | location=[[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates={{Coord|33|53|N|135|9|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] | Historical Rank=One of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} | Established=Unknown | Designation=Gobo City Designated Historic Site }} '''Ueno Ōji''' (上野王子) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Gobō, Wakayama|Gobo]], [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]], Japan. It is one of the {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} shrines. The site of the shrine is marked with a sign for Ueno Ōji at the outskirts of the Ueno settlement in Nata-chō, just before the bridge crossing the Uenogawa River along the old road near [[:en:Japan_National_Route_42|Japan National Route 42]]. It was designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on May 8, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/20.html | title=Site of Ueno Ōji | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == History == While the exact founding date of Ueno Ōji is unknown, it appears in historical records dating back to the early 12th century. In the {{ill|Chūyūki|ja|中右記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūyūki|qq=}}, an entry dated October 20, 1109 (Tennin 2) mentions a purification ritual performed at Ueno Slope. The shrine is first explicitly mentioned as "Next, Ueno Ōji" in an entry from October 10, 1201 (Kennin 1) in the "Kumano Dō no Aimagoki" (included in the {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}). Another record of a pilgrimage visit appears in the {{ill|Minkeiki|ja|民経記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minkeiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広橋経光|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} dated April 26, 1210 (Jōgen 4).Heibonsha [1997: 105] In early modern records, it is noted that a Buddhist well (仏井戸) located along the Oguri Kaidō within a field was considered the original site of the shrine (as mentioned in the {{ill|Kiizoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiizoku Fudoki|1=Kiizoku Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604964}}). Three Buddhist statues—[[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]], and [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]]Nishi [1987: 129]—located at the bottom of the well are believed to be the honji (本地仏) of the shrine. Pilgrims frequently visited this site, as noted in the {{ill|Kii Meisho Zue|ja|紀伊名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Buddhist well still exists along the national road, and it is traditionally cleaned twice a year. The Buddhist well was also designated as a Gobo City Historic Site on July 18, 1969.{{Cite web | publisher=Gobo City Board of Education | url=http://www.city.gobo.wakayama.jp/gobo/1120ka/bunkazai/21.html | title=Buddhist Well | accessdate=2009-09-27 }} == See Also == * {{ill|Kumano Faith|ja|熊野信仰|1=Kumano Faith|2=ja|3=熊野信仰|WD=Q135501741}}{{efn|The religion of [[Kumano Shrines]]|1=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Three Shrines|lt=Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Faith|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野三山}} * [[:en:Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodō]] - {{ill|Kiiji|ja|紀伊路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|ja|九十九王子|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|2=ja|3=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|4=fr|5=Kujūkuōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} : {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|ja|九十九王子 (御坊市)|en|lt=Shioya Ōji|error=九十九王子 (御坊市)|oji=王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Gobo City)|qq=}} * '''Ueno Ōji''' - {{ill|List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|ja|九十九王子 (印南町)|lt=Tsu'i Ōji|error=List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|oji=王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines (Inami Town)|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == '' Nishi, Ritsu (1987).
